title: Britain's Looming Timber Crisis
subtitle: 不种树的英国，正在酿成一场建筑危机

paragraphs.en: The UK is facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood, Confor has warned. The forestry and wood trade body has called for urgent action to reduce the country's reliance on timber imports and provide a stable supply of wood for future generations. Currently only 20 percent of the UK's wood requirement is home-grown while it remains the second-largest net importer of timber in the world.
paragraphs.zh: 林业和木材贸易机构Confor警告称，由于未能种植足够的树木来生产木材，英国未来将面临建筑危机。该机构呼吁采取紧急行动，减少国家对木材进口的依赖，并为子孙后代提供稳定的木材供应。目前，英国仅有20%的木材需求来自本土，同时它仍是世界第二大木材净进口国。

paragraphs.en: Coming at a time of fresh incentives from the UK government for landowners to grow more trees, the trade body says these don't go far enough and fail to promote the benefits of planting them to boost timber supplies. 'Not only are we facing a carbon crisis now, but we will also be facing a future construction crisis because of a failure to plant trees to produce wood,' said Stuart Goodall, chief executive of Confor.
paragraphs.zh: 正值英国政府出台新激励措施鼓励土地所有者种植更多树木之际，该贸易机构表示这些措施力度不够，且未能宣传种植树木以增加木材供应的好处。Confor首席执行官斯图尔特·古道尔说：“我们现在不仅面临碳危机，而且由于未能种植树木来生产木材，我们未来还将面临建筑危机。”

paragraphs.en: The UK has ideal conditions for growing wood to build low-carbon homes and is a global leader in certifying that its forests are sustainably managed, Confor says. While around three quarters of Scottish homes are built from Scottish timber, the use of home-grown wood in England is only around 25 percent. The causes of the UK's current position are complex and range from outdated perceptions of productive forestry to the decimation of trees by grey squirrels.
paragraphs.zh: Confor表示，英国拥有种植木材建造低碳住宅的理想条件，并在证明其森林得到可持续管理方面处于全球领先地位。虽然约四分之三的苏格兰房屋是用苏格兰木材建造的，但英格兰本土木材的使用率仅为25%左右。英国当前处境的原因很复杂，从对生产性林业的过时观念到灰松鼠对树木的大量破坏都有。

words.meaning: n. 大量毁灭，严重破坏

paragraphs.en: It also encompasses significant hesitation on behalf of farmers and other landowners to invest in long-term planting projects. While productive tree planting can deliver real financial benefits to rural economies, the focus of government support continues to be on food production and the rewilding of native woodland solely for biodiversity. Goodall added: 'We need our land to also provide secure supplies of wood for construction and contribute to net-zero.'
paragraphs.zh: 这还包括农民和其他土地所有者对投资长期种植项目的严重犹豫。虽然生产性植树能为农村经济带来真正的经济利益，但政府支持的重点仍然是粮食生产以及纯粹为了生物多样性而进行的原生林地再野化。古道尔补充道：“我们需要我们的土地也能为建筑提供安全的木材供应，并为净零排放做出贡献。”
